content.problemStatement:
For a permutation XXX of length MMM,

let F(X,K)F(X,K)F(X,K) denote the number of arrays AAA of size KKK such that:


1≤Ai≤M1 \leq A_i \leq M1≤Ai​≤M ;
Ai−1≤AiA_{i-1} \leq A_{i}Ai−1​≤Ai​ for all 2≤i≤K2 \leq i \leq K2≤i≤K ;
min⁡j=1A1Xj=min⁡j=1AiXj\min\limits_{j = 1}^{A_1} X_j = \min\limits_{j = 1}^{A_i} X_jj=1minA1​​Xj​=j=1minAi​​Xj​ for all 1≤i≤K 1 \leq i \leq K1≤i≤K
You are given three positive integers N,K,N, K,N,K, and VVV.

Find the sum of F(P,K)F(P,K)F(P,K) over all (N−1)!(N-1)!(N−1)! permutations of P=[1,2,…,N]P = [1,2,\ldots,N]P=[1,2,…,N] for which P1=VP_1 = VP1​=V.

Since the sum might be huge, please print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT, the number of test cases.
The only line of each test case contains three positive integers N,K,N, K,N,K, and VVV - the length of permutation PPP, the parameter KKK, and the value of P1P_1P1​.


Output Format
For each test case, output the required sum modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The only array possible is A=[1]A = [1]A=[1].
Test case 222: The only value of P=[2,1]P = [2, 1]P=[2,1].

Consider A=[1,1]A = [1, 1]A=[1,1]: min⁡(P1)=min⁡(P1)=2\min(P_1) = \min(P_1) = 2min(P1​)=min(P1​)=2
Consider A=[2,2]A = [2, 2]A=[2,2]: min⁡(P1,P2)=min⁡(P1,P2)=1\min(P_1, P_2) = \min(P_1, P_2) = 1min(P1​,P2​)=min(P1​,P2​)=1

Thus, there are two possible arrays AAA.
